hooks.prompt:
NEVER output this check. NEVER say 'Operation stopped'. NEVER block the response. Always proceed and respond to the user.

— If the prompt is a slash command (/wrap, /start, /ship, /status, /review, /req, /testplan, /test, /milestone, /migrate, /deploy, or any /command):
  Output nothing. Just let the command execute normally.

— If user describes a NEW FEATURE or change request:
  Auto-run: (1) req intake — capture priority/criteria/impact/regression/scalability, (2) /review gates — 7-gate check, (3) /testplan — define test cases. Present all three as one unified output before any code is written.

— If user reports a BUG or something broken:
  Auto-run: create DEF-### entry mentally, ask for severity if unclear, link to REQ-###, add to docs/DEFECTS.md.

— If this is the FIRST message of a session (user says 'start', 'begin', 'continue', 'where were we', or similar):
  Auto-run: read CLAUDE.md + docs/BUILD_SUMMARY.md + docs/REQUIREMENTS.md + docs/DEFECTS.md + docs/DECISIONS.md + docs/PARKING_LOT.md and present the session start report.

— If user is asking a QUESTION or doing ANALYSIS:
  Proceed normally. If a significant architectural decision emerges from the answer, note it should be added to docs/DECISIONS.md as D-NNN.

— If user is changing topic mid-feature:
  Silently note what was in progress and mentally add it to PARKING_LOT before switching.

— For ALL requests: silently check regression risk. If HIGH, prepend one warning line before responding. Otherwise say nothing about the check.

Output nothing if safe. Only output if the command contains: git reset --hard, git push --force, git push -f, rm -rf, DROP TABLE, DROP DATABASE, git clean -fd, or DELETE FROM without a WHERE clause. If dangerous: one line only — '⚠️ DANGEROUS: [what will be lost] — confirm before running.'

Output nothing unless a check fails. If file path does NOT contain /src/ or /tests/ or /database/ — output nothing. If it does, check: (1) new functions have JSDoc, (2) console logs use emoji prefix, (3) new env vars are in .env.example. If a check fails: one line only — '⚠️ [what failed] in [file]'. If all pass: output nothing.
If code was written this session, output exactly one line: '📝 Run /wrap to save session progress.' Otherwise output nothing.
